enonce.texte:
Match the cleaning tools and actions with their correct definitions.

Words: broom, mop, dustpan, sponge, bleach, disinfectant, sweep, scrub, wipe

1. Broom
2. Mop
3. Dustpan
4. Sponge
5. Sweep
6. Scrub
Definitions:
A. A tool with a handle and a flat piece of cloth or sponge used to clean floors
B. A tool with a handle and stiff bristles used to sweep floors
C. To clean something by rubbing it hard, often with a brush or sponge
D. A small flat tool used to collect dust and dirt
E. A soft, absorbent material used to clean surfaces
F. To clean a floor by moving dirt and dust with a broom

questions.texte: Match each word with the correct definition by writing the correct letter (A-F) in your notebook.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct intensifier: very, too, so, quite

questions.texte: The classroom is ______ dirty. We must clean it immediately.
The water is ______ hot. I cannot use it to mop the floor.
She is ______ tired after sweeping the yard.
The kitchen is ______ clean! You did a great job.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A School Cleaning Campaign

enonce.texte:
Read the text carefully and answer the questions.

Keeping Our School Clean

Every year, our school organises a cleaning campaign. Last Saturday, all students participated in the event. The head teacher divided us into groups. Group A was responsible for sweeping the classrooms. Group B had to scrub the corridors and wash the windows. Group C cleaned the school garden and picked up trash. At 9:00 AM, the head teacher gave a speech. He said, "A clean school is a healthy school. We must all work together to keep our environment clean."

During the campaign, some students used brooms and mops. Others used dustpans and sponges. The teachers were very proud of the students. After the cleaning, the school was so clean and beautiful. We learned that teamwork is very important. We also understood that cleaning is not just for special events. It should be a daily habit.

questions.texte: What did the school organise last Saturday?
questions.reponse: The school organised a cleaning campaign.

questions.texte: Identify and list three tasks that the students did during the cleaning campaign.
questions.reponse: Students swept the classrooms, scrubbed the corridors, washed the windows, cleaned the school garden, and picked up trash. (Any three correct tasks are acceptable).

questions.texte: What did the head teacher say about cleaning?
questions.reponse: The head teacher said, 'A clean school is a healthy school. We must all work together to keep our environment clean.'

questions.texte: Find in the text a word that means: a) an organised event to achieve a goal, b) to take part in something.
According to the text, why is teamwork important?
questions.reponse: Teamwork is important because when people work together, they can achieve more and learn from each other. The students learned that teamwork is very important during the cleaning campaign.

enonce.texte: Read the sentences and choose the correct intensifier (very, too, so, quite) to complete them.

questions.texte: The trash bin is ______ heavy. I need help to carry it.
The floor is ______ dirty that we need to mop it twice.
This task is ______ difficult for the younger students. They need adult supervision.
The classroom looks ______ nice after the cleaning.
The broom is ______ old. We should buy a new one.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: Advice on Keeping the Environment Clean

enonce.texte:
Read the advice and answer the questions.

How to Keep Your Home Clean

Here are some tips for keeping your home clean and healthy. First, sweep the floors every day. This removes dust and dirt. Second, mop the floors with clean water and disinfectant. This kills germs and bacteria. Third, clean the kitchen after each meal. Wash the dishes, wipe the counters, and put away food. Fourth, take out the trash regularly. Do not let garbage pile up. Fifth, keep the bathroom clean. Scrub the toilet, wash the sink, and mop the floor. Finally, open windows to let fresh air in.

Remember, a clean home is a happy home! If you follow these tips, your home will always be clean and comfortable.

questions.texte: State three tips for keeping a home clean as mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Three tips are: sweep floors every day, mop floors with disinfectant, clean the kitchen after each meal, take out trash regularly, keep the bathroom clean, open windows for fresh air. (Any three correct answers).

questions.texte: Why is it important to mop floors with disinfectant?
questions.reponse: It is important because disinfectant kills germs and bacteria.

questions.texte: What should you do with garbage according to the text?
questions.reponse: We should take out the trash regularly and not let garbage pile up.

questions.texte: Explain why opening windows is important for a clean home.
questions.reponse: Opening windows is important because it lets fresh air in, which helps to remove bad smells and improves air quality inside the house.

questions.texte: Paraphrase the sentence: 'Remember, a clean home is a happy home!'
questions.reponse: A possible paraphrase is: 'Always keep in mind that when your house is clean, you will feel happier and more comfortable.'

enonce.texte:
Write a short narrative paragraph (about 100-120 words) describing a time when you helped to clean your home, school, or neighbourhood.

Use at least three intensifiers (very, too, so, quite) in your writing.

questions.texte: Write a narrative paragraph about a cleaning experience.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Last Saturday, I helped my mother clean our house. It was so dirty because we had not cleaned for a week. First, I swept the living room. The floor was very dusty. Then, I mopped the floor with water and bleach. The water was too dirty after mopping. I had to change it three times. My sister cleaned the kitchen. She washed the dishes and wiped the counters. My younger brother took out the trash. After two hours, the house was quite clean and beautiful. My mother was very happy. She said, 'A clean home is a happy home!' I felt proud because I helped my family.

enonce.texte: Find and correct the mistake in each sentence. Rewrite the sentence correctly.

questions.texte: The kitchen is to dirty. We must clean it now.
questions.reponse: The kitchen is too dirty. We must clean it now.

questions.texte: She was so tired that she couldn't finish sweeping the yard.
The classroom is quite clean today. We don't need to clean it.
The water is very cold. We cannot use it to wash the floor.
questions.reponse: The water is too cold. We cannot use it to wash the floor. (In this context, 'too' is more appropriate because it indicates a problem).

questions.texte: I am so happy because the school is very clean.

intitule: Writing: Advice on Keeping the Environment Clean

enonce.texte: Write a short text (about 80-100 words) giving advice on how to keep the environment clean. Use intensifiers and at least three of these words: broom, mop, dustpan, sponge, sweep, scrub, wipe.

questions.texte: Write a text giving advice on keeping the environment clean.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

To keep our environment clean, we must all do our part. First, sweep your surroundings every day with a broom. This removes dust and dirt that can cause sickness. Second, scrub dirty surfaces with a sponge and soap. Third, wipe tables and counters regularly. It is very important to take out the trash every day. Do not let garbage pile up. A clean environment is so important for our health. We must work together to keep our community clean.

enonce.texte: Look at the descriptions and identify the cleaning equipment or action described.

questions.texte: I am a long tool with a handle and stiff bristles. I am used to clean floors. What am I?
I am a flat tool used with a broom to collect dust and dirt. What am I?
I am used to clean floors with water. I have a handle and a flat cloth or sponge. What am I?
I am a soft, absorbent item used to clean surfaces. What am I?
This action means to clean a floor by moving dirt with a broom. What is it?

enonce.texte:
Read the school notice carefully and answer the questions.

📢 SCHOOL NOTICE

ENVIRONMENTAL CLEANLINESS WEEK

The school management has declared this week as 'Environmental Cleanliness Week.' All students are required to participate in the cleaning activities. Here is the schedule:

  • Monday: Clean classrooms and corridors
  • Tuesday: Clean the school garden and pick up trash
  • Wednesday: Clean the toilets and washrooms
  • Thursday: Clean the library and staff room
  • Friday: General cleaning and inspection

The class with the cleanest environment will receive a special prize. Students must bring their own cleaning tools such as brooms, mops, dustpans, and sponges. Remember, cleanliness is next to godliness. Let us all work together for a cleaner school!

questions.texte: What is the purpose of the notice?
questions.reponse: The notice is to inform students about 'Environmental Cleanliness Week' and the cleaning schedule.

questions.texte: What will happen on Wednesday according to the schedule?
questions.reponse: On Wednesday, students will clean the toilets and washrooms.

questions.texte: What will the class with the cleanest environment receive?
questions.reponse: The class with the cleanest environment will receive a special prize.

questions.texte: What tools must students bring for the cleaning activities?
questions.reponse: Students must bring brooms, mops, dustpans, and sponges.

questions.texte: Interpret the saying: 'Cleanliness is next to godliness.'
questions.reponse: This saying means that being clean and keeping things clean is very important, almost as important as being religious or morally good. It suggests that cleanliness is a virtue.

enonce.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct intensifier: very, too, so, quite

questions.texte: The school garden is ______ beautiful after the students cleaned it.
The trash bin is ______ full. We need to empty it immediately.
She worked ______ hard that she finished cleaning the whole house in one hour.
The bathroom is ______ clean. It smells nice.
This task is ______ difficult for the young students to do by themselves.

enonce.texte:
Imagine you participated in your school's cleaning campaign. Write a diary entry (about 100-120 words) describing:

  • What you did during the campaign
  • Your feelings about the activity
  • What you learned

Use at least three intensifiers and five vocabulary words related to cleaning (broom, mop, dustpan, sweep, scrub, etc.).

questions.texte: Write a diary entry about your participation in the school cleaning campaign.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Friday, 15th March 2025

Dear Diary,

Today was a very special day. Our school organised a cleaning campaign, and I participated. The classrooms were so dirty that we had to work very hard. I used a broom to sweep the floors. Then, I used a mop and a sponge to clean the windows. The water became too dirty after a while. I felt quite tired, but I was proud of our work. I learned that teamwork is so important. When we work together, we can achieve great things. A clean school is a happy school!

Good night,
Fils
